headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,800 AS US-IRAN TALKS CALM INFLATION FEARS; Analysts Eye Path to $5,000
snippet: Gold steadied near $4,818/oz Wednesday after a 1.1% session drop as the US and Iran prepare a second round of diplomatic talks before a two-week ceasefire deadline. Weaker dollar, persistent central bank buying, and geopolitical risk continue to underpin the trend, with major banks maintaining $5,000 price targets for 2026.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD SILVER SETS Q1 RECORD: 787,000 oz Ag Produced, Up 76% Year-Over-Year
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (USAS) delivered record consolidated Q1 2026 silver production of 787,000 oz — a 76% jump versus Q1 2025 — driven by Cosalá operations. The company remains on track for full-year guidance of 3.2–3.6 Moz at US$30–35/oz AISC.

headline: SILVER'S SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT: Institute Forecasts 46 Moz Shortfall in 2026 as Physical Investment Surges
snippet: The Silver Institute's World Silver Survey 2026 projects a sixth consecutive annual supply deficit of 46.3 Moz — up 15% from 2025 — as physical investment demand rose 14% to 217.7 Moz. Total above-ground stocks have absorbed 762 Moz in drawdowns since 2021, and supply is forecast to fall a further 2% this year.

headline: CHINA RARE EARTHS SURGE 44%: Northern Rare Earth Sets Q2 at 38,804 Yuan/t — More Than Double Year-Ago Levels
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth raised its Q2 concentrate price 44.6% to 38,804 yuan/tonne — more than double the level a year ago — as Beijing's planned sulfuric acid export ban from May compounds earlier rare earth export restrictions. Chinese RE stocks jumped up to 8.5% on the news.

headline: COPPER HITS $6.07/LB, 6-WEEK HIGH as US and Iran Prepare Second-Round Ceasefire Talks
snippet: Copper futures climbed toward $6.1/lb April 15, fully recovering losses since the start of the Middle East conflict, as the US and Iran prepare a second round of talks before the two-week ceasefire expires. The metal is up 30% year-over-year on structural demand from EVs, data centers, and grid electrification.

headline: CHINA'S PBOC BUYS GOLD 17TH STRAIGHT MONTH: Record 5 Tonnes Added in March, Reserves Hit All-Time High
snippet: China's PBOC purchased 5 tonnes of gold in March 2026 — its largest single-month addition since February 2025 — extending its buying streak to 17 consecutive months with total reserves now at a record high. Global central banks are on pace to purchase roughly 850 tonnes in 2026.

headline: HARVEST GOLD MAPS 50 TARGETS ON 32km ABITIBI CORRIDOR After 105 g/t Visible Gold Discovery
snippet: Harvest Gold (HVG.V) has defined 50 drill targets along a 32-kilometre Kiask River corridor in Quebec's Urban Barry Belt, building on the Mosseau central discovery that returned 105 g/t Au with visible gold. Eight priority holes within 500m of the discovery hole are planned as the 2026 drill campaign gets underway.

headline: INDIA-ZAMBIA COBALT AND COPPER TALKS COLLAPSE: 9,000 sq km Allocation Stalled Over Mining Rights
snippet: India's critical minerals push hit a wall as Zambia stalled bilateral negotiations over mining rights assurances, jeopardizing India's allocation of 9,000 sq km for cobalt and copper exploration. The impasse is a setback for New Delhi's strategy to build supply chains independent of China.

headline: TRUMP STRIPS UPPER PECOS WATERSHED OF MINING PROTECTIONS, Opening New Mexico to Exploration Wave
snippet: The Trump administration canceled mineral withdrawal protections around the Upper Pecos watershed in New Mexico, triggering a wave of new mining proposals and the prospect of litigation from environmental groups. The rollback is part of a broader federal push to open protected land to critical mineral exploration.

headline: GOLD ABOVE $4,800 WITH DOLLAR AT 6-WEEK LOW; Silver Grinds Into Critical $80 Resistance — GoldSeek
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian metals update flags gold consolidating above $4,800 as the DXY falls to a 6-week low, with markets pricing only a 30% probability of Fed cuts. Silver is testing the key $80/oz resistance level, with a clean breakout historically preceding sharp follow-through moves in mining equities.
